title: Parathelypteris glandulifera
taxonomy: Parathelypteris glandulifera is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Parathelypteris, which belongs to the division Pteridophyta. This genus is a subset of the family Thelypteridaceae, which consists of about 1,200 species of ferns. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the order Polypodiales and further classified within the class Polypodiopsida. The kingdom to which Parathelypteris glandulifera belongs is Plantae, which includes all flowering plants, conifers, and other vascular plants.

properties: Parathelypteris glandulifera, also known as the glandular strap fern, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its rich content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. intro: Parathelypteris glandulifera, also known as the glandular lady fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Parathelypteris glandulifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Parathelypteris glandulifera is often made into teas, infusions, or dried extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
